zh.lifeSummary: 當Roger de Beauchamp於1362年9月14日出生在英國英格蘭貝德福爾布勒左時，他的父親Roger de Beauchamp27歲，母親Joan Clopton22歲。他和Mary至少有1個兒子。他去世於1406年5月13日在英國英格蘭維爾爾索爾茲伯里享年43歲，埋葬在英國維爾爾費黑頓 安格。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Roger de Beauchamp于1362年9月14日出生在英国英格兰貝德福爾布勒左时，他的父亲Roger de Beauchamp27岁，母亲Joan Clopton22岁。他和Mary至少有1个儿子。他去世于1406年5月13日在英国英格兰維爾爾索爾茲伯里享年43岁，埋葬在英国維爾爾費黑頓 安格。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Beauchamp
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Antoine, Jacques, Yves, Andre, Armand, Emile, Fernand, Marcel, Marthe, Remi, Alcide, Camille.

English (of Norman origin) and French: habitational name from any of several places in France, for example in Manche and Somme, that are named with Old French beu, bel ‘fair, lovely’ + champ(s) ‘field, plain’ (from Latin bello campo ‘beautiful field’). In English the surname is generally pronounced Beecham . Compare Bushaw .
English: in some cases, an Anglo-Norman French adaptation of Scottish Campbell , by translation into French of the folk etymology de campo bello ‘from the fair field’.

variants.name: Beecham
meanings: English (Lincolnshire): variant of   Beauchamp  , reflecting the normal English pronunciation.

variants.name: Beachem
meanings: English (Somerset and Gloucestershire): variant of   Beauchamp  .

variants.name: Beachum
meanings: Altered form of English   Beauchamp  .

variants.name: Bagent
meanings: English:
possibly a colloquial pronunciation of Beauchamp or Beecham . Old French Beau- often appears as Be(e)- in English surnames. The -ch- has been voiced and excrescent -t has been added.

perhaps an Anglicized form of a French or Flemish surname such as Beaujean and Bejean, from Old French bel Jehan ‘handsome John’ + excrescent -t. It could have been introduced to England in the 15th or 16th century by Flemish migrants.

variants.name: Beacham
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Beauchamp  .

variants.name: Campbell
meanings: Scottish: nickname from Gaelic cam ‘crooked, bent’ + beul ‘mouth’. As a result of folk etymology, the surname was often represented in Latin documents as de bello campo ‘of the fair field’, which led to the name sometimes being ‘translated’ into Anglo-Norman French as Beauchamp .
Irish (North Armagh): adopted for Gaelic Mac Cathmhaoil ‘son of Cathmhaol’ (literally ‘battle chief’): see Caulfield and Cowell .
English: variant of   Camel  , under the influence of the Scottish name (see 1 above).

variants.name: Bushaw
meanings: Americanized form of French   Beauchamp   and   Bouchard  .

variants.name: Beacom
meanings: English (Somerset) and Irish (Fermanagh): of Norman origin, a variant of   Beauchamp  .

nameMeanings.name: Roger
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French personal name, Rog(i)er, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, from hrōd ‘fame’ + gār, gēr ‘spear’. This was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain, replacing the native Old English form Hrōðgār. Roger was one of the most popular boys' names throughout the medieval period, but less so after the Reformation, though it has continued in regular use to the present day. Roger, Count of Sicily ( c.1031–1101 ), son of Tancred, recovered Sicily from the Arabs. His son, also called Roger, ruled Sicily as king, presiding over a court noted for its splendour and patronage of the arts.

variants.name: Rodger
meanings: Variant spelling of   Roger  , in part from the surname derived from the given name in the Middle Ages.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Roger de Beauchamp was born on 14 September 1362, in Bletsoe, Bedfordshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Roger de Beauchamp, was 27 and his mother, Joan Clopton, was 22. He had at least 1 son with Mary. He died on 13 May 1406, in Salisbury, Wiltshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 43, and was buried in Fisherton Anger, Wiltshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Roger de Beauchamp est né le 14 septembre 1362, à Bletsoe, Bedfordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Roger de Beauchamp, avait 27 ans et sa mère, Joan Clopton, avait 22 ans. Il a eu au moins 1 fils avec Mary. Il est décédé le 13 mai 1406, à Salisbury, Wiltshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 43 ans, et a été enterré à Fisherton Anger, Wiltshire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Roger de Beauchamp am 14. September 1362 in Bletsoe, Bedfordshire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater Roger de Beauchamp 27 und seine Mutter Joan Clopton 22. Er hatte mit Mary mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb am 13. Mai 1406 im Alter von 43 Jahren in Salisbury, Wiltshire, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Fisherton Anger, Wiltshire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Roger de Beauchamp è nato il il 14 settembre 1362 nel Bletsoe, Bedfordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, Roger de Beauchamp, aveva 27 anni e il suo madre, Joan Clopton, aveva 22 anni. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio con Mary. Morì il 13 maggio 1406, nel Salisbury, Wiltshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 43 anni, e fu sepolto a Fisherton Anger, Wiltshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Roger de Beauchampが1362年9月14日にイギリスイギリスベドフォードシュアイアブレトソで生まれた時，父Roger de Beauchampは27歳，母Joan Cloptonは22歳であった。彼はMaryとの間に少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。彼は1406年5月13日にイギリスイギリスウィルトシュアイアサリスブリーで43歳で死去し，イギリスウィルトシュアイアフィシェルトン アンジャーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Roger de Beauchamp(이)가 1362년 9월 14일 영국 잉글랜드 베드퍼드실르 블레츠에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Roger de Beauchamp(은)는 27세였고, 그의 어머니 Joan Clopton(은)는 22세였다. 그는 Mary(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1406년 5월 13일 영국 잉글랜드 윌츠히르 살리스버이에서 43세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 윌츠히르 피셔턴 앤거에 묻혔다.

pt.14 September 1362–13 May 1406: 14 de setembro de 1362–13 de maio de 1406
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Roger de Beauchamp nasceu em 14 de setembro de 1362, em Bletsoe, Bedfordshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Roger de Beauchamp, tinha 27 anos e sua mãe, Joan Clopton, tinha 22 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho com Mary. Ele faleceu em 13 de maio de 1406, em Salisbury, Wiltshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 43 anos, e foi sepultado em Fisherton Anger, Wiltshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Roger de Beauchamp родился 14 сентября 1362 г. в местности блецо, бедфордшир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Roger de Beauchamp было 27 лет, а матери, которую звали Joan Clopton, 22. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын от Mary. Он умер 13 мая 1406 г. в местности салисбури, уилцхир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 43 лет и был похоронен в местности фиширтон ангир, уилцхир, Англия.

es.14 September 1362–13 May 1406: 14 de septiembre de 1362–13 de mayo de 1406
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Roger de Beauchamp nació el 14 de septiembre de 1362, en Bletsoe, Bedfordshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Roger de Beauchamp, tenía 27 años y su mamá, Joan Clopton, tenía 22 años. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo con Mary. Murió el 13 de mayo de 1406, en Salisbury, Wiltshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 43 años, y fue sepultado en Fisherton Anger, Wiltshire, Inglaterra.
